title: edit_mode in notebook is validated as string against literal slice instead of enum
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
SOLID-L / Type Safety

## Location
src/tools/file/notebook.rs:72-84 (edit_mode extraction + validation) plus 165, 249, 266-274 (string matches)

## Violation
```rust
let edit_mode = args.get("edit_mode").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("replace");
if !["replace", "insert", "delete"].contains(&edit_mode) {
    return (format!("Invalid edit_mode '{edit_mode}'. Must be 'replace', 'insert', or 'delete'."), true);
}
...
match edit_mode { "replace" => { ... } "insert" => { ... } "delete" => { ... } _ => unreachable!() }
```

Same pattern as TodoItem.status: stringly-typed, validated against a hardcoded list, then matched against the same list three more times (lines 165, 249, 266-274). The `_ => unreachable!()` is a tell — the type system COULD prove this branch dead but cannot because the type is &str. Future addition of a "swap" edit_mode requires editing four locations.

## Mandated refactor
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
enum EditMode { Replace, Insert, Delete }
```
Parse via serde from the args. Every subsequent match is exhaustive without `unreachable!()`. Cross-references issue #973 (TodoStatus enum refactor) — same root cause.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
